title: Sagina ciliata

This family consists of many other species of flowering plants commonly known as pink or carnation family. intro: Sagina ciliata, also known as Marsh Dew or Hair-stem Campion, is a perennial herb native to wetlands in Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating urinary tract issues and reducing swelling. The bioactive constituents of Sagina ciliata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
